summary: Add the SERP Analysis Agent with typed schemas, docs-aligned prompt construction, injected safe JSON completion, and deterministic heuristic fallback.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable SERP Analysis Agent module exists under agents/ with typed input, output, signal, Zod schemas, and a stable public function for analyzing SERP results.
The agent builds a prompt aligned with docs/08_ai_agents_and_prompts.md Agent 2 and calls the existing safeJsonCompletion() utility through an injected LlmClient when one is provided.
The agent provides a deterministic heuristic fallback that analyzes mock SERP-like results without external API keys, process.env reads, network calls, database writes, or paid services.
The analysis output includes serpWeaknessSummary, weakSignals, strongSignals, and a clamped 0-100 serpWeaknessScoreHint.

The fallback recognizes generic articles, forum/community results, PDF/government/old-page/poor-UX signals, missing interactive tools, mature SaaS/tool competition, and empty SERP results in a deterministic way.

Focused automated tests cover LLM success, malformed or rejected LLM output falling back to heuristics, score clamping/normalization, generic article/no-tool weak signals on mock SERP, strong competition signals, and empty SERP behavior.
README documents the SERP Analysis Agent slice, heuristic fallback behavior, no-secret/no-network policy, and the test command.

No opportunity analysis, scoring engine, scan orchestration, database persistence, API route, UI, auth, cron, deployment, real LLM provider wiring, SERP provider changes, or paid service setup is implemented.
notes: Derived from docs/08_ai_agents_and_prompts.md Agent 2 prompt/schema and docs/11_development_plan.md M3 agent foundation.
systems_architect is required because this extends the agents/ boundary and feeds later Opportunity Analysis and scoring work.
Keep this task side-effect free. Persistence, scan orchestration, scoring, and API integration belong to later M4 tasks.
non_goals: No Opportunity Analysis Agent, scoring engine, MVP Spec Agent, or scan orchestrator.
No SerpResult or Opportunity database writes and no Prisma/lib changes.
No /api/scans/run route, dashboard integration, or UI changes.
No real LLM provider configuration, environment-variable secret loading, real network calls in tests, or paid service setup.
No changes to services/llm or services/serp contracts.
